title: Exponential Growth, Decay, and Logarithmic Scales · Worked Case: Exponential Factors
summary: Model adoption, cooling, depreciation, and sound-level measurements. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int exponential factors increment in an exponential and logarithmic scenario lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish additive change from multiplicative growth and derive period factors.
Compare nonlinear candidates using residual patterns, simplicity, mechanism, and validation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Exponential Factors evidence
steps.instruction: $1100 grows once per year at 4.5% for 2 years. Calculate the compounded value. Use the result to decide Exponential Factors for an exponential and logarithmic scenario lab.
steps.why: The Exponential Factors result for an exponential and logarithmic scenario lab is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
